title: Tier Tagger
description: Display PvP Tiers in Nametags! Supports Tier Tests, MCTiers, PVPTiers, and SubTiers.
body:
# Tier Tagger

TierTagger is a mod that displays player tiers in-game. It supports Minecraft 1.8.9 Forge and 1.21.x + 26.1.x Fabric & Quilt. Gamemodes from [Tier Tests](https://tiertests.com/), [MCTiers](https://mctiers.com/), [PVPTiers](https://mctiers.com/), and [SubTiers](https://subtiers.net/) are available on their respective versions.

## Information
The default key for the config screen is **N**, for the nearest player screen **R**, and for the last hit player screen **C**. All can be changed in the keybinds settings.

### Features

- `Tab list tiers` — Show tier tags in the player tab list
- `Closest player lookup` — View nearest player’s tier
- `Last hit lookup` — View last hit player’s tier
- `HUD lookup panel` — Top-left panel showing skin, name, region, rank, and all gamemode tiers with peak
- `Player search` — Search any username across all APIs, showing region, rank, points, and per-mode tiers with peak
- `Config screen` — In-game GUI with live nametag preview using your nametag
- `Multi-API support` — Tier Tests, MCTiers, SubTiers, PVPTiers
- `Cross-API fallback` — Automatically queries other APIs when data is missing

### Configuration

- `Tier Source` — API to use (Tier Tests, MCTiers, SubTiers, PVPTiers)
- `Gamemode` — Gamemode to display (loaded dynamically per source)
- `Display Mode` — Highest Fallback, Highest Always, Selected Only, or Ranking
- `Cross-API Fallback` — Same Mode or Any Mode
- `Icon Type` — Classic Unicode or MCTiers PNG icons
- `Display Type` — Tier tag position on nametags, Prefix or Suffix
- `Show Region` — Display region after tier
- `Show in Tab` — Show tier tags in tab list
- `MCTiers Format` — Use LT5/HT1 vs F/S

**NOTE**: Some features may not work with specific clients or on specific servers. If this happens to you, please contact **@flxmeyt** on Discord with information (server ip, what feature isn't working, etc), and I will attempt to resolve the issue.
